title: couralia fluviatilis
intro: Couralia fluviatilis, commonly known as Water Couralia or River Couralia, is a plant species in the family Rutaceae, native to tropical regions of the Americas. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which offer various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic actions, as well as the ability to help lower fever and alleviate pain. The therapeutic actions of Couralia fluviatilis are attributed to its bioactive constituents, which include alkaloids, terpenes, and flavonoids, such as couraline and rutin, that work together to exhibit antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and analgesic properties. The bioactive constituents of Couralia fluviatilis have been used in traditional medicine to treat various conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections.
benefits: Couralia fluviatilis has many benefits, such as its ability to lower cholesterol levels and reduce the risk of heart disease, making it an excellent natural remedy for individuals with cardiovascular issues. This plant has also been found to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, asthma, and other inflammatory conditions, improving the overall quality of life for those affected. Additionally, Couralia fluviatilis has been shown to possess antibacterial and antifungal properties, making it a valuable asset in the treatment of wounds and infections, as well as in the prevention of digestive issues such as diarrhea and ulcers. By incorporating Couralia fluviatilis into one's lifestyle, individuals can experience a range of health benefits that can lead to improved mental and physical well-being, increased energy levels, and a reduced risk of chronic diseases.
side_effects: Couralia fluviatilis can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to its high concentration of alkaloids. In severe cases, improper use can lead to cardiovascular problems, including rapid heartbeat and decreased blood pressure, which may result in dizziness and fainting. Additionally, long-term exposure to the plant's toxic compounds can cause neurological damage, manifesting as confusion, tremors, and loss of coordination. Prolonged use of couralia fluviatilis without proper medical supervision may also lead to kidney and liver damage.
